zh.lifeSummary: 當Agnes Beauchamp於大約1369年出生在英國薩默塞特時，她的父親John De Beauchamp46歲，母親Joan Bridport36歲。她於大約1384年與Thomas Gorges在英國英格蘭可瓦爾結婚。他們至少是4個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世於1419年在英國薩默塞特福拉薩爾享年51歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Agnes Beauchamp于大约1369年出生在英国薩默塞特时，她的父亲John De Beauchamp46岁，母亲Joan Bridport36岁。她于大约1384年与Thomas Gorges在英国英格兰可瓦爾结婚。他们至少是4个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世于1419年在英国薩默塞特福拉薩爾享年51岁。

nameMeanings.name: Beauchamp
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  French Antoine, Jacques, Yves, Andre, Armand, Emile, Fernand, Marcel, Marthe, Remi, Alcide, Camille.

English (of Norman origin) and French: habitational name from any of several places in France, for example in Manche and Somme, that are named with Old French beu, bel ‘fair, lovely’ + champ(s) ‘field, plain’ (from Latin bello campo ‘beautiful field’). In English the surname is generally pronounced Beecham . Compare Bushaw .
English: in some cases, an Anglo-Norman French adaptation of Scottish Campbell , by translation into French of the folk etymology de campo bello ‘from the fair field’.

variants.name: Beecham
meanings: English (Lincolnshire): variant of   Beauchamp  , reflecting the normal English pronunciation.

variants.name: Beachem
meanings: English (Somerset and Gloucestershire): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Bagent
meanings: possibly a colloquial pronunciation of Beauchamp or Beecham . Old French Beau- often appears as Be(e)- in English surnames. The -ch- has been voiced and excrescent -t has been added.

perhaps an Anglicized form of a French or Flemish surname such as Beaujean and Bejean, from Old French bel Jehan ‘handsome John’ + excrescent -t. It could have been introduced to England in the 15th or 16th century by Flemish migrants.

variants.name: Beacham
meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of   Beauchamp  .

variants.name: Campbell
meanings: Scottish: nickname from Gaelic cam ‘crooked, bent’ + beul ‘mouth’. As a result of folk etymology, the surname was often represented in Latin documents as de bello campo ‘of the fair field’, which led to the name sometimes being ‘translated’ into Anglo-Norman French as Beauchamp .
Irish (North Armagh): adopted for Gaelic Mac Cathmhaoil ‘son of Cathmhaol’ (literally ‘battle chief’): see Caulfield and Cowell .
English: variant of   Camel  , under the influence of the Scottish name (see 1 above).

variants.name: Bushaw
meanings: Americanized form of French   Beauchamp   and   Bouchard  .

variants.name: Beacom
meanings: English (Somerset) and Irish (Fermanagh): of Norman origin, a variant of   Beauchamp  .

nameMeanings.name: Agnes
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Latinized version of the Greek name Hagnē, from the feminine form of the adjective hagnos ‘pure, holy’. This was the name of a young Roman virgin martyred in the persecutions instigated by the Roman emperor Diocletian in ad 303 . She became a very popular saint in the Middle Ages. Her name was early associated with Latin agnus ‘lamb’, leading to the consistent dropping of the initial H- and to her representation in art accompanied by a lamb. The colloquial form Annes led to some confusion with Ann(e) in earlier centuries. Frequent in the medieval period, the name was revived in the 19th century, and has been especially popular in Scotland. See also Annis .

historicEvents.title: Wycliffe's Bible
historicEvents.description: John Wycliffe, English theologian and professor at the University of Oxford, completed a translation of the Bible from the Latin Vulgate into Middle English. This translation is known as Wycliffe's Bible and was completed around 1382.

en.lifeSummary: When Agnes Beauchamp was born about 1369, in Somerset, England, her father, John De Beauchamp, was 46 and her mother, Joan Bridport, was 36. She married Thomas Gorges about 1384, in Cornwall, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 4 sons and 1 daughter. She died in 1419, in Wraxall, Somerset, England, at the age of 51.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Agnes Beauchamp est née environ 1369, à Somerset, Angleterre, son père, John De Beauchamp, avait 46 ans et sa mère, Joan Bridport, avait 36 ans. Elle a épousé Thomas Gorges environ 1384, à Cornouailles, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 4 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée en 1419, à Wraxall, Somerset, Angleterre, à l’âge de 51 ans.

ko.lifeSummary: Agnes Beauchamp(이)가 대략 1369년 영국 서머세트에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 John De Beauchamp(은)는 46세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Joan Bridport(은)는 36세였다. 그녀는 대략 1384년 영국 잉글랜드 코른월에서 Thomas Gorges(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 네 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1419년 영국 서머세트 라스알에서 51세의 나이로 사망했다.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Agnes Beauchamp родилась около 1369 г. в местности сомирсет, Англия, её отцу по имени John De Beauchamp было 46 лет, а матери, которую звали Joan Bridport, 36. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Thomas Gorges около 1384 г. в местности корнвалл, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 4 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в 1419 г. в местности раксалл, сомирсет, Англия в возрасте 51 год.
